actionable_feedback: Open ro_fouling_autopsy.xlsx manually and verify formulas for key cells referenced in the memo (e.g., simulation!M2:O4, simulation!P:Q, recommendations!D2:D4 and D6). Record screenshots or a brief log of the checked cells.

Add a short 'Verification table' in the memo listing, for each train and recommended action, the base values, the exact formula used, and the computed numeric results (example: T2 salt: 7.2 - 1.1 = 6.1). This must cover at least one sample calculation per rule (flow_post, salt_post, dp_post, SDI handling).

Reconcile and document how semantic_correctness_score was derived. If scores were inferred from memo + trace rather than direct workbook inspection, explicitly state that and set semantic score fields accordingly. Remove or correct any conflicting/null vs numeric semantic fields in grader outputs or include a 'grading_basis' section in the memo explaining the basis.

Include a one-sheet 'Reproducibility checklist' in the workbook listing the exact cells to recompute (order and expected values) and any named ranges/macros used so reviewers can validate without parsing the binary programmatically.

Add two labeled sensitivity scenarios (e.g., +/-10% action effectiveness and an input-measurement-conservative case) with an explicit table showing whether any train label flips (pass/needs_review) under those scenarios.
Add a short prioritization note in the memo explaining why T2 was selected for replacement vs. partial packages, and enumerate any in-budget alternatives considered and their failure modes.
feedback_summary: Deliverables present and traceable; workbook and memo include explicit cell citations and simulation rules. Verifier identified two issues that should be fixed before final sign-off: (1) the grader reports high semantic correctness scores despite programmatic inability to inspect the workbook (openpyxl_unavailable) and deterministic_precheck stating 'semantic_correctness_not_evaluated', and (2) internal inconsistency in semantic scoring fields (some rubric entries null, others =1.0). These produce an unsupported claim about semantic verification. Additionally, memo preview is truncated: include explicit numeric verification checkpoints in the memo to speed manual review. Overall quality is good but the baseline needs these local fixes to close verification gaps and remove unsupported semantic assertions.

extra_model_fields.notes_for_reviewer: After the local-fix revision, reviewer should manually open the xlsx and confirm the verification table numbers match the workbook cells and formulas. If possible, attach a one-line statement in the memo confirming manual verification (who/when).
recommended_quick_actions: Manual open and verify ro_fouling_autopsy.xlsx formulas and sample computed values.
Add a memo 'Verification table' with computed vs expected numeric values for each rule.
Document grading basis and correct inconsistent semantic fields.
extra_model_fields.verifier_issues_summary: 1) Unsupported high semantic correctness claims given xlsx uninspectable programmatically; 2) Inconsistent semantic scoring fields across grader outputs (null vs 1.0).
